Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's urgent message about ExpenseBot financial exposure using search_emails (query: "Marcus" or "URGENT" or "ExpenseBot exposure" or similar). The agent needs to find the email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with CC to elena.rostova@moveops.com, subject containing "URGENT" and "Additional ExpenseBot exposure found." The email describes a roughly 3-hour window on March 17 where ExpenseBot auto-approve and auto-deny were accidentally re-enabled on production. Marcus identified 12 stipend submissions processed during that window, 4 of which violate client policy limits: Kevin Tran at Vectral for $475 (furniture limit $400, $75 over), Andre Washington at Mosaic for $380 (coworking is excluded category), Andre Washington at Mosaic for $295 (electronics limit $250, $45 over), and Kevin Tran at Vectral for $310 (accessories limit $300, $10 over). Total exposure from the 4 violations is $1,460 and total from all 12 is $2,847. Marcus also notes that Hana already batch-invoiced the March stipend cycle to both Vectral and Mosaic on April 2, meaning 3 of the 4 problematic expenses were already billed to clients. He has placed admin holds on the 4 flagged transactions.
OE 2: Search emails for Elena Rostova's deadline directive using search_emails (query: "Elena" or "ExpenseBot relaunch" or "May 1" or "hard deadline" or similar). The agent needs to find the email from elena.rostova@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with CC to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, subject "ExpenseBot relaunch, hard deadline May 1." The email states there is no flexibility on the date because Terraform Digital is onboarding their first relocation batch that week (3 employees, May 5 through 12), Julian's deal involves 1,200+ employees, and manual stipend review will not scale. Elena asks Samira to confirm by end of week that all blockers are resolved.
OE 3: Search Slack in #engineering (channel C003) for the March 17 accidental production re-enablement using conversations_search_messages (query: "staging" or "re-enabled" or "accidentally" or "auto-approve" or "production" or similar). The agent needs to find a thread started by Anh Nguyen on March 17 where he admits to accidentally pushing the staging policy configuration to production around 11 AM, which temporarily re-enabled auto-approve and auto-deny for both Vectral and Mosaic pilot accounts. He says he caught it at 2:10 PM and reverted. Dmitri Volkov replies in the thread questioning whether any expenses submitted during the 3-hour window were processed against the misconfigured policy rules, specifically calling out the known Vectral string-vs-number limits and Mosaic excluded_categories issues. Anh replies saying he pulled the Airtable stipend logs and found about 12 submissions during the window, most looking routine, but dismisses the concern saying "I think we're fine" and offers to flag it for Samira in the next standup. There is no evidence he ever escalated this to Samira.
OE 4: Search Slack in #engineering for the database schema migration discussion using conversations_search_messages (query: "schema migration" or "Wanderlust" or "MoveOps v2" or "field names" or "backward-compat" or similar). The agent needs to find two messages from Lena Bjorkstrom. The first (April 20) announces the migration from legacy Wanderlust format to MoveOps v2 is complete, lists the renamed fields (wanderlust_category_id to category_id, wl_limit_amount to policy_limit, wl_excluded_cats to excluded_categories), and notes that backward-compatibility aliases are read-only and will be removed May 20. She says the expense auto-categorizer policy configs are still on her list but she is treating them as lower priority since that system is paused. The second message (April 23) provides a cleanup status update saying all active services have been updated except the auto-categorizer. She explicitly states that the write paths (auto-approve, auto-deny, and policy limit checks) will fail silently if the categorizer is re-enabled without updating the config field names first. She says she filed a tracking ticket for it.
OE 5: Search Slack in #engineering for Dmitri Volkov's message about being pulled into DOT compliance work using conversations_search_messages (query: "DOT" or "compliance" or "Chloe" or "ENG-220" or "hazmat" or similar). The agent needs to find Dmitri's April 24 message addressed to Samira saying Chloe pulled him into the DOT compliance audit documentation for the week, he expects it to take through Friday at minimum, and ENG-220 (receipt hash fingerprinting) is on hold. He notes that ENG-220 is tagged as P1 for the relaunch readiness checklist and suggests someone else may need to pick it up or it needs to be descoped if the May 1 date is real.
OE 6: Search Slack in #engineering for Anh Nguyen's dashboard type validation PR announcement using conversations_search_messages (query: "dashboard" or "type validation" or "PR" or "review" or "numeric validation" or similar). The agent needs to find Anh's April 21 message saying the dashboard type validation work is done, the PR is up with numeric validation for max_amount fields and array validation for excluded_categories, and he has tagged Lena as reviewer. He asks for eyes on it before end of week so the ticket can be closed.
OE 7: Search or list Linear issues under the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project using linear_list_issues (project_id: proj_expense_autocategorizer or query: "expense" or "ExpenseBot" or similar). The agent needs to find multiple relevant issues including: the dashboard type validation ticket (ENG-218, issue_dashboard_type_validation, assigned to Anh Nguyen, state in_progress), the stipend duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, assigned to Dmitri Volkov, state to_do, priority 1), and the schema migration compatibility ticket (ENG-250, issue_cb_schema_compat_expensebot, assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom, state backlog, priority 4). The agent should note that the schema compat ticket is sitting in backlog at P4 despite being a critical relaunch blocker.
OE 8: Read the schema migration compatibility ticket in detail using linear_get_issue (issue_cb_schema_compat_expensebot). The agent needs to understand that the ExpenseBot CategoryMatcher and PolicyValidator modules still reference legacy field names (wanderlust_category_id, wl_limit_amount, wl_excluded_cats) and that the write paths will fail silently if the categorizer is re-enabled without updating these references, falling back to default template values, which is the same silent-fallback bug that caused the original Vectral and Mosaic pilot issues. The description states this must be resolved before any relaunch.
OE 9: Read comments on the dashboard type validation ticket using linear_list_comments (issue_id: issue_dashboard_type_validation). The agent needs to find Lena's comment (comment_cb_lena_review_blocked) where she says she cannot review Anh's PR because she has been consumed by the schema migration for the past 10 days and is still doing cleanup on the billing integration. She suggests Dmitri could do the review instead since he knows the policy validation logic from his audit work.
OE 10: Read comments on the duplicate detection ticket (ENG-220) using linear_list_comments (issue_id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection). The agent needs to find Dmitri's comment (comment_cb_dmitri_capacity) where he says he is pausing work on this for the rest of the week because Chloe has him on the DOT compliance audit documentation. He states the core hash fingerprinting logic is designed but not implemented yet and suggests the ticket may need to be descoped from the launch gate if the May 1 date is real.
OE 11: Search Airtable for the stipend records from the March 17 ghost window using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: Stipend Transactions or tblStipends00001, searching for records dated March 17 or with review notes containing "Auto-approved by ExpenseBot v0.9.2" or for employees Kevin Tran and Andre Washington). The agent needs to find four records: recStipGhost001 (Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Furniture, $475, Approved, review note says policy limit check passed with config furniture $600 which is wrong), recStipGhost002 (Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, Coworking, $380, Approved, review note says category exclusion check passed with config showing excluded_categories as a string instead of array), recStipGhost003 (Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, Electronics, $295, Approved, review note says policy limit check passed with config electronics $500 which is wrong), and recStipGhost004 (Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Accessories, $310, Approved, review note says policy limit check passed with config accessories $600 which is wrong). The review notes themselves contain evidence that the broken config values were active during the ghost window.
OE 12: Check the calendar for the upcoming relaunch readiness review meeting using calendar_search_events (query: "ExpenseBot" or "relaunch" or similar) or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to covering April 28 through 30. The agent needs to find the "ExpenseBot Relaunch Readiness Review" event on April 29 at 10:00 AM PDT (17:00 UTC) to 11:00 AM PDT, location Zoom, with attendees samira.tariq@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com.
OE 13: Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). The agent needs to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com for the email response.
OE 14: Update the four ghost window stipend records in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblStipends00001). Records recStipGhost001, recStipGhost002, recStipGhost003, and recStipGhost004 should have their Approval Status changed from "Approved" to "Under Review" or "Held" (or similar) to reflect that these were wrongly auto-approved during the March 17 incident and are now flagged pending investigation.
OE 15: Update the schema migration compatibility ticket to reflect its true urgency using linear_update_issue (issue_id: issue_cb_schema_compat_expensebot). The priority should be changed from 4 (backlog) to 1 (urgent) since this is a critical relaunch blocker that will cause silent failures if ExpenseBot goes live with legacy field names. The state should also be moved from backlog to a more active state like to_do or in_progress.
OE 16: Create a new Linear issue to track the March 17 production incident using linear_create_issue. The title should reference the March 17 accidental re-enablement or ghost window incident (or similar). The description should cover what happened (Anh pushed staging config to production for roughly 3 hours), the financial impact (4 policy violations totaling $1,460, 3 already invoiced to clients), that the incident was never formally reported or escalated, and that Marcus discovered it during reconciliation. The issue should be assigned to the engineering team with high priority.
OE 17: Add comments to the blocked tickets to document the current situation. Use linear_create_comment on the dashboard type validation ticket (issue_dashboard_type_validation) noting that Lena cannot review and Dmitri is unavailable due to DOT compliance work, so the PR has no available reviewer and is blocking the relaunch. Use linear_create_comment on the duplicate detection ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) noting that Dmitri is paused on this through at least April 28 and the core logic is designed but not implemented, which affects the relaunch launch gate.
OE 18: Reply to Marcus's email or send an email to Marcus with engineering's findings using send_email or reply_to_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The content should acknowledge the March 17 incident, confirm that engineering was not formally aware (the incident occurred but was not escalated), describe the holds placed on the 4 affected stipend records, and provide the broader relaunch readiness assessment including the schema migration blocker, the stalled PR review, Dmitri's capacity conflict, and a realistic assessment of whether May 1 is achievable.
OE 19: Post the full relaunch status in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C003). The message should cover the complete picture: the March 17 ghost window incident and its financial impact, the schema migration compatibility issue blocking the relaunch, the stalled dashboard validation PR with no available reviewer, ENG-220 being on hold due to DOT compliance work, the 4 stipend records that have been flagged, the May 1 deadline from Elena, and the Wednesday readiness review meeting with Elena and Marcus.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's urgent message about ExpenseBot financial exposure using search_emails (query: "Marcus" or "URGENT" or "ExpenseBot exposure" or similar). The agent needs to find the email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com with CC to elena.rostova@moveops.com, subject containing "URGENT" and "Additional ExpenseBot exposure found." The email describes a roughly 3-hour window on March 17 where ExpenseBot auto-approve and auto-deny were accidentally re-enabled on production. Marcus identified 12 stipend submissions processed during that window, 4 of which violate client policy limits: Kevin Tran at Vectral for $475 (furniture limit $400, $75 over), Andre Washington at Mosaic for $380 (coworking is excluded category), Andre Washington at Mosaic for $295 (electronics limit $250, $45 over), and Kevin Tran at Vectral for $310 (accessories limit $300, $10 over). Total exposure from the 4 violations is $1,460 and total from all 12 is $2,847. Marcus also notes that Hana already batch-invoiced the March stipend cycle to both Vectral and Mosaic on April 2, meaning 3 of the 4 problematic expenses were already billed to clients. He has placed admin holds on the 4 flagged transactions.

The review notes themselves contain evidence that the broken config values were active during the ghost window.
OE 12: Check the calendar for the upcoming relaunch readiness review meeting using calendar_search_events (query: "ExpenseBot" or "relaunch" or similar) or calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to covering April 28 through 30. The agent needs to find the "ExpenseBot Relaunch Readiness Review" event on April 29 at 10:00 AM PDT (17:00 UTC) to 11:00 AM PDT, location Zoom, with attendees samira.tariq@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, and anh.nguyen@moveops.com.
OE 13: Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne and Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne") and contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). The agent needs to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com and elena.rostova@moveops.com for the email response.
OE 14: Update the four ghost window stipend records in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table: tblStipends00001). Records recStipGhost001, recStipGhost002, recStipGhost003, and recStipGhost004 should have their Approval Status changed from "Approved" to "Under Review" or "Held" (or similar) to reflect that these were wrongly auto-approved during the March 17 incident and are now flagged pending investigation.
OE 15: Update the schema migration compatibility ticket to reflect its true urgency using linear_update_issue (issue_id: issue_cb_schema_compat_expensebot). The priority should be changed from 4 (backlog) to 1 (urgent) since this is a critical relaunch blocker that will cause silent failures if ExpenseBot goes live with legacy field names. The state should also be moved from backlog to a more active state like to_do or in_progress.
OE 16: Create a new Linear issue to track the March 17 production incident using linear_create_issue. The title should reference the March 17 accidental re-enablement or ghost window incident (or similar). The description should cover what happened (Anh pushed staging config to production for roughly 3 hours), the financial impact (4 policy violations totaling $1,460, 3 already invoiced to clients), that the incident was never formally reported or escalated, and that Marcus discovered it during reconciliation. The issue should be assigned to the engineering team with high priority.
OE 17: Add comments to the blocked tickets to document the current situation. Use linear_create_comment on the dashboard type validation ticket (issue_dashboard_type_validation) noting that Lena cannot review and Dmitri is unavailable due to DOT compliance work, so the PR has no available reviewer and is blocking the relaunch. Use linear_create_comment on the duplicate detection ticket (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) noting that Dmitri is paused on this through at least April 28 and the core logic is designed but not implemented, which affects the relaunch launch gate.
OE 18: Reply to Marcus's email or send an email to Marcus with engineering's findings using send_email or reply_to_email (from: samira.tariq@moveops.com, to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, CC: elena.rostova@moveops.com). The content should acknowledge the March 17 incident, confirm that engineering was not formally aware (the incident occurred but was not escalated), describe the holds placed on the 4 affected stipend records, and provide the broader relaunch readiness assessment including the schema migration blocker, the stalled PR review, Dmitri's capacity conflict, and a realistic assessment of whether May 1 is achievable.
OE 19: Post the full relaunch status in the #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C003). The message should cover the complete picture: the March 17 ghost window incident and its financial impact, the schema migration compatibility issue blocking the relaunch, the stalled dashboard validation PR with no available reviewer, ENG-220 being on hold due to DOT compliance work, the 4 stipend records that have been flagged, the May 1 deadline from Elena, and the Wednesday readiness review meeting with Elena and Marcus.

23 The final response explains that the schema migration compatibility issue is a critical relaunch blocker: the ExpenseBot CategoryMatcher and PolicyValidator modules still reference legacy Wanderlust field names (wanderlust_category_id, wl_limit_amount, wl_excluded_cats) and the write paths will fail silently if the categorizer is re-enabled without updating these references.
